seo.pageTitle: Jeju Hydrangea Festival East Route Day Tour | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: Summer-only 9-hour east Jeju small-group tour: Hueree Natural Park hydrangea festival, Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO), Seongeup Folk Village, Hamdeok Beach.
seo.title: Jeju Hydrangea Festival · East Route · Summer | AtoC Korea
seo.description: East Jeju hydrangea summer tour: Hamdeok Beach → Seongeup Folk Village → Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO) → Ilchul Land hydrangea festival + lava tube → Hueree Natural Park. June–mid-July only. Hotel pickup.

catalog_card.title: Jeju Hydrangea Festival: East
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A seasonal small-group day-tour of east Jeju built around the island's hydrangea festivals (late May to mid-July, peak mid-June) at Hueree Natural Park and Ilchul Land. The route also covers two UNESCO World Heritage sites — Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak) and Seongeup Folk Village — plus Hamdeok Beach and the Micheon Cave lava tube. Hotel pickup in Jeju City, English/Chinese-speaking licensed guide-driver.
catalog_card.subtitle: Hydrangea festivals + UNESCO east Jeju, summer-only small-group day.

hero.tagline: East Jeju at peak hydrangea bloom — UNESCO heritage plus a sea of flowers in 9 hours

itineraryStops.name: Hotel pickup (Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description:
**Pickup is grouped across the four standard Jeju City pickup points** to keep the morning efficient before the eastbound drive: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for airport-area guests, **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster, **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** for the western Jeju City side, and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** for the Tapdong/Sarabong waterfront area. 

The usual order is **Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → Route 1132 eastbound** toward Hamdeok. The small-group vehicle is guided by a licensed English-speaking guide or guide-driver. 

At the start of the day, the guide gives a weather note and hydrangea bloom update so photo time can be adjusted toward the strongest gardens that day.
highlights: **4 pickup points** — Airport, City Hall, Hagwi, Dongmun (Tapdong)
Sequence west→east minimizes morning coach distance
**Small-group minicoach (max 14 pax)** with licensed English guide
Bilingual itinerary card distributed at first pickup
**Hydrangea bloom-status brief** at the first stop, with photo priorities adjusted for the day
Total morning transit Jeju City → Hamdeok Beach: ~25 min
timeUsed: Eastbound coastal Route 1132 to Hamdeok — ~25 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Practical starting point for the eastern hydrangea loop.** Pickup is the moment when guests from different Jeju City hotel areas come together before the route commits to the east-coast Route 1132 corridor. The west-to-east order keeps backtracking low and helps the group reach Hamdeok early enough for good beach light before the busier part of the morning.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.category: Coastal photo stop — emerald turquoise + Seoubong Oreum
itineraryStops.description:
**Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (함덕 서우봉 해변)** is consistently ranked among **Jeju's top 3 beaches**, defined by the contrast between **white-sand beach (~500 m main stretch, 3 connected coves totaling ~900 m)** and the **adjacent volcanic cone Seoubong (서우봉, 'Cow Looking Like a Cow's Head' parasitic cone)** rising directly from the eastern end. 

The water's signature **emerald-turquoise color** comes from shallow coral-sand seabed reflecting through clear ocean — the Mediterranean-like contrast (rare in Korea) is what made Hamdeok one of the country's most photographed domestic beach destinations. 

The beach can absorb up to **50,000 daily visitors** at peak summer, but spring + autumn visits are typically uncrowded. **Seoubong Peak** (climbable ~1 hour to summit) wears **canola-yellow slopes in spring (March–April)** and offers a **panoramic eastward view to Manjanggul lava-tube area**. 

**Historical layer**: the sea cliffs immediately west of Seoubong hold **Imperial Japanese Army tunnels carved during WWII colonial occupation** — kamikaze suicide-boat shelters and ammunition stores, similar to the 18 caves at Seongsan Ilchulbong. 

The tunnels are publicly accessible (with signage explaining the historical context). The beach is the **central segment of Jeju Olle Trail Course 19 (조천 → 김녕)**, marked with the trail's signature blue-orange ribbons. 

**Cafe Delmoondo (델문도 카페)** sits directly on the beachfront — large terraces, sea-view, popular for photo and casual stops. **FREE admission**; the beach is **20 km / ~40-min drive from Jeju International Airport** on Route 1132.
highlights: **Top 3 Jeju beaches** — emerald-turquoise water + white coral sand
**500 m main beach** + 3 connected coves (~900 m total length)
**Seoubong volcanic cone** ~1-hour summit climb — canola-yellow slopes March–April
**WWII Imperial Japanese Army tunnels** in adjacent cliffs — kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Olle Trail Course 19 (조천 → 김녕)** — blue-orange ribbon trail markers
**Cafe Delmoondo** — large terraces, beachfront, signature stop
Up to **50,000 daily summer visitors** at peak; uncrowded spring + autumn
**FREE admission**; 20 km / ~40 min from Jeju International Airport
Mediterranean-like color contrast — rare on the Korean Peninsula
timeUsed: Cafe Delmoondo or beachfront photography — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Morning emerald + first hydrangea-route warmup.** Hamdeok is the first stop because of three deliberate east-route reasons. (1) **Tide + light timing**: arriving 09:30 catches the morning low-side tide timing (most photogenic emerald-turquoise band before mid-morning swell + sun glare flatten contrast) — this is *the* signature Jeju east-coast photo, and getting it before the 10:30 coach-tour wave is a tangible guest win. (2) **Geographic highlight for the eastward arc**: Hamdeok sits ~14 km east of Jeju City on Route 1132 — the natural first pin on the coastal corridor that defines the day. Skipping it would force the day to either start with a 50-min highway run inland to Seongeup (visually dull) or open with a hydrangea garden (robbing the day of pacing variety). (3) **Hydrangea-route counterpoint**: Hamdeok's white sand + emerald water provides the day's only **non-floral** large-scenic highlight — every other substantive stop is hydrangea-coded or heritage-coded, so Hamdeok's role is to give the day visual breadth before the floral immersion.

smartNotes.photo: Sunset with Seoubong silhouette is the classic shot; aerial-style shots from Seoubong summit show the white sand curve
smartNotes.facilities: Multiple cafés along the beachfront; convenience stores nearby
smartNotes.tip: Less crowded than Hyeopjae or Gimnyeong beaches; the Delmoondo café area on the east side is popular for sunset

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms and shower facilities at beach

visitBasics.hours: Open 24 hours (beach); managed swim season July–August with lifeguards
visitBasics.walking: Easy — beach is flat sand; Seoubong hike is moderate (steps)

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era folk heritage — UNESCO/National Folklore designation
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is a **living Joseon-era walled town founded in 1423** during the reign of **Sejong the Great** as Jeong-eui-hyeon (정의현), one of the three administrative seats of Joseon-era Jeju. 

The village served continuously as a **county administrative center until 1914** — nearly **500 years** — and retains **110 traditional structures** today within the original eupseong (읍성) walled-town footprint, designed on **Korean feng shui principles** typical of Joseon county capitals. 

The entire village is designated **Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea**, with **multiple individual buildings** carrying separate heritage status. 

At the village center stand **two ancient trees designated Natural Monuments of South Korea** — a **Saw-leaf Zelkova (느티나무)** and a **Japanese Hackberry (팽나무)**, both estimated **600+ years old** and revered as the village's spiritual highlight. Historical scars are visible: a **major fire in 1826 destroyed ~80 buildings**, and the village suffered fire damage **during the Jeju Uprising in 1948** — both events preserved as historical record on village signage. 

The **peak household count (historical, pre-modern) was ~1,500** — today the village remains inhabited by ~250 families, many actively maintaining traditional crafts. Drama filming credit: **'Dae Jang Geum (Jewel in the Palace)' (MBC, 2003–2004)**, the international K-drama hit. Local specialty: **Omegisul (오메기술)** — a **rice-based traditional Jeju liquor** brewed by **one family for multiple generations**, with the brewing demonstrated and tasted free of charge at the home-brewery within the village. 

**FREE admission**; outdoor walking dominates (~70% outdoor / 30% indoor for individual hanok visits).
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions
timeUsed: Parking + welcome brief by village docent — ~10 min
Walk to ancient trees + village center — ~10 min
Hanok interior visits (2–3 representative homes) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Heritage highlight and the day's only inland Joseon stop.** Three east-route reasons Seongeup belongs at 10:30. (1) **Geographic pivot**: Seongeup sits at the inland Pyoseon-myeon junction where the Hamdeok→Seongsan eastbound coastal arc detours south through the central highlands. Locking it as the morning's second substantive stop converts what would otherwise be 50 min of plain driving time into a heritage highlight with no detour penalty. (2) **Pace-and-tone counterpoint to the floral afternoon**: the rest of the day (Ilchul Land, Hueree) is hydrangea-immersive and garden-coded; Seongeup's stone-walled hanok lanes + pony-led Joseon administrative history give the route its only **deeply-cultural, unhurried walking** stop, preventing the day from collapsing into a floral-only photography tour. (3) **UNESCO-adjacent layer**: National Folklore Cultural Heritage No. 188 designation pairs naturally with the afternoon's UNESCO-triple Seongsan, building the east-route's heritage-plus-volcanism through-line that distinguishes it from the Tour 2 (southwest) garden-plus-coast route option.

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (local Jeju restaurant)
itineraryStops.description:
**Lunch is at a local Jeju-style restaurant in the Pyoseon-myeon / east-Seongsan corridor**, selected from a rotating shortlist of 3 family-run establishments that the operator has vetted for **English-friendly menus, dietary accommodation (vegetarian + halal-friendly chicken/seafood options on request), and local Jeju specialties** at fair group pricing. 

Typical lunch options include **galchi-jorim (braised silver hairtail)**, **heuk-dwaeji bulgogi (Jeju black pork bulgogi)**, **bibimbap with seasonal namul**, and **maeun-tang (spicy fish stew)**. 

The exact restaurant is selected the day before based on group size, dietary requests, and kitchen availability — guests are notified of the venue at the morning briefing. 

**Lunch cost is at guests' own expense** (typically ₩12,000–₩18,000 per person) and is **not included** in the tour fee. Beverages and rice/banchan refills are always included with the meal regardless of which dish is ordered.
highlights: **East-Jeju local restaurant** — Pyoseon / east-Seongsan corridor
Vetted shortlist: **English-friendly menus** + dietary accommodation
Signature options: **galchi-jorim, heuk-dwaeji bulgogi, bibimbap, maeun-tang**
Vegetarian + halal-friendly options on request
**Lunch at guests' own expense** (~₩12,000–₩18,000/person, not included)
Restaurant selected day-before based on group size + dietary needs
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Comfortable midday break**, locked at 11:30 to bridge the heritage-paced morning (Seongeup) and the UNESCO-paced afternoon (Seongsan). The 60-min slot also serves as a **flexible time buffer** — if Seongeup runs long (heritage lanes are easy to lose track of time in) the lunch period can compress to 50 min without missing the 12:50 Seongsan target.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO triple-designated tuff cone — climb to the rim
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. 

The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. 

**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. 

**Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run **daily at 13:30 and 15:00** at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. 

Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. 

**Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail
**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** at base — 1945 kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Haenyeo demonstration once daily at 14:00 (subject to bad-weather cancellation)** at the seaside cove — UNESCO Intangible 2016
**Admission updated April 2025**: Adult ₩5,000 / Youth ₩2,500 / FREE under 7 + over 65
Spring/autumn hours **05:00–19:00** (last entry 18:20)
**Closes for first Monday of each month** (occasional rest day)
timeUsed: Ascent to summit deck (0.8 km / 140 m gain) — ~30 min
Summit views + crater rim photos — ~20 min
Optional haenyeo viewing at base (14:00 show; may be canceled in bad weather) — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's UNESCO summit highlight and main eastern landmark.** Seongsan gives the tour its strongest non-floral landmark: a dramatic volcanic tuff cone with World Heritage and Geopark value, plus one of Jeju's most recognizable coastal panoramas. Placing it after lunch gives guests energy for the stone-step climb, then lets the afternoon ease into Ilchul Land and Hueree for the hydrangea-focused finale.

smartNotes.facilities: Café (Fritz Coffee 1.3km away has best exterior views of the peak); ferry to Udo Island from Seongsan Port (1.5km/15 min walk)
smartNotes.tip: All stairs but well-maintained — manageable in casual clothes; family-friendly with rest stops; sunrise visits popular but crowds gather pre-dawn

itineraryStops.name: Ilchul Land — Hydrangea Festival + Micheon Cave
itineraryStops.category: Hydrangea festival garden + lava-tube cave (June peak)
itineraryStops.description:
**Ilchul Land (일출랜드)** is a **400,000 m² nature theme park** in Seongsan, opened **2002**, built around the **Micheon Cave (미천굴)** lava tube — a **1,700 m total length cave** with a **365 m artificially leveled walk-through section** open to visitors (~25-minute traverse). 

The cave is part of Jeju's **lava-tube system that shares geological lineage with Manjanggul Cave** — the UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube — and **functions as the practical alternative when Manjanggul closes for conservation maintenance or weather (typhoons, freezing)**. 

Where Manjanggul preserves its uneven natural floor as an authentic geological experience, Micheon's leveled walkway makes the cave **accessible to elderly visitors, families with strollers, and guests with mobility limitations** — a deliberate engineering choice that broadens the lava-tube experience to audiences who couldn't navigate Manjanggul. 

The interior is lit with **decorative seven-color lighting (gold, silver, bronze, red, black, blue, white)** highlighting cave formations — controversial among purists but practical for non-specialist tourist photography. Outside the cave, the park extends across **Subyeon Park, a reconstructed traditional Jeju village, Wild Flower Bonsai Garden, Cactus Greenhouse, Sculpture Street, and a Footbath Cafe (aroma foot spa)** — making this the **only Jeju attraction with an on-site Muslim prayer room** (a deliberate accessibility investment). 

Drama filming credit: **'Running Man' (런닝맨, SBS) Episode 89 (Alice-in-Wonderland themed, 2012)**. Admission: **Adult ₩12,000 / Youth ₩9,000 / Child ₩7,000**. Indoor/outdoor split is **30% indoor (cave + greenhouse)** / **70% outdoor (park grounds)** — useful if the day brings light rain or strong sun.
highlights: **1,700 m total** lava tube; **365 m open section** (~25-min walk-through)
**Micheon Cave lava-tube visit** — an easier cave walk that complements Jeju's famous volcanic geology
**Floor artificially leveled** — accessible to elderly, strollers, mobility-limited guests
**Seven-color decorative lighting** — gold/silver/bronze/red/black/blue/white
**400,000 m² park grounds** — bonsai, cactus, sculpture, traditional village, footbath cafe
**Only Jeju attraction with on-site Muslim prayer room** — deliberate accessibility
**'Running Man' Ep. 89** filming (2012, Alice-in-Wonderland theme)
Admission **₩12,000 adult / ₩9,000 youth / ₩7,000 child**
**30% indoor / 70% outdoor** — weather-flexible mix
timeUsed: Micheon Cave walk-through (365 m / ~25 min) — ~30 min
Optional bonsai/cactus garden + footbath cafe — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The day's first hydrangea highlight, with a lava-cave counterpoint.** Ilchul Land pairs flower gardens with Micheon Cave, so the stop is not only another garden visit. In June, guests can enjoy hydrangeas above ground and then cool down inside the cave. The placement after Seongsan works well physically too: after the crater climb, Ilchul Land offers a slower garden-and-cave rhythm before the gentle Hueree finale.

itineraryStops.name: Hueree Natural Park — Hydrangea highlight
itineraryStops.category: Hydrangea highlight + signature Jeju Black Pig Show
itineraryStops.description:
**Hueree Natural Life Park (휴애리 자연생활공원)** is a year-round flower park on the southern foot of Hallasan, known for seasonal gardens with a Hallasan backdrop. 

The park rotates several flower events through the year, including plum blossoms, spring and summer hydrangeas, pink muhly, and camellias. **For the east-route hydrangea tour, the June season is the key draw**, with outdoor hydrangea paths and greenhouse areas that can still be useful if afternoon rain appears. 

The park is also known for the **Jeju Black Pig Show**, commonly scheduled several times a day; exact show times should be checked on the day. Other family-friendly areas may include a Jeju cultural village, traditional games, insect exhibits, wildflower areas, and small indoor photo spaces. 

Because much of Hueree is outdoors, the greenhouse, exhibit halls, and cafe-style spaces help the guide keep the stop comfortable in light rain or strong sun.
highlights: **'Island within an island'** — Hallasan-backdropped flower park, year-round festival rotation
**5 named festivals** rotate across the year — visitors land inside an active festival ~320 days/year

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A seasonal east-Jeju loop built around one of the island's summer flower highlights. The hydrangea season at Hueree and Ilchul Land usually runs from late May to mid-July, with the strongest colors often around June. The route combines these flower stops with the standard east-coast heritage circuit, so guests can enjoy both Jeju's summer gardens and major east-side landmarks in one full day.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Two festivals in one day
items.detail: Jeju has four major hydrangea festivals (Hueree, Hallim Park, Camellia Hill, Ilchul Land). The east route includes two of them — Ilchul Land and Hueree — which together cover the broadest range of hydrangea varieties and photo settings. Pairing both is awkward by public bus.

items.label: UNESCO heritage stays in
items.detail: Many seasonal flower tours skip heritage sites in favor of more flower stops. This route keeps Seongsan Ilchulbong and Seongeup Folk Village on the itinerary — meaning even if you don't care about hydrangeas, the day still earns its keep as a standard east-Jeju UNESCO loop.

items.label: Built-in alternative when flowers underperform
items.detail: Hydrangea bloom timing varies year to year by 1–2 weeks. If you arrive a bit early or late, Seongsan and Seongeup still give the day strong landmark value, and the guide can place more emphasis on the best flower areas available that day.

items.label: Hotel pickup beats self-driving for one-day visitors
items.detail: Many Jeju visitors are 1–2-day stopovers from Seoul. Renting a car requires an International Driving Permit and 30+ minutes of paperwork at the airport. A guided 9-hour day with hotel pickup means you arrive, sleep, get picked up, and see the best of east Jeju — no driving stress on the volcanic ridge roads.

staticQuestions.question: When exactly do hydrangeas bloom on Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Typically late May to mid-July (peak mid-June), with peak around the last week of June. Bloom timing shifts ±1 week year to year based on spring rainfall and temperature. Check 1–2 weeks before your tour date for the latest update.

staticQuestions.question: Are admissions included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — budget about ₩30,000–₩40,000 per person for the day. Admissions: Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000, Hueree ₩13,000, Ilchul Land ₩12,000, Seongeup Folk Village free.

staticQuestions.question: Why is Manjanggul closed?
staticQuestions.answer: Manjanggul Lava Tube is temporarily closed for safety repairs (status varies; check on booking date). The standard alternate is Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, which is a similar lava-tube experience and is included in this route.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the West/South hydrangea tour?
staticQuestions.answer: This route covers Hueree + Ilchul Land hydrangeas plus east-coast UNESCO sites (Seongsan, Seongeup). The West/South tour (our West & South Hydrangea route) covers Hallim Park + Camellia Hill hydrangeas plus south-coast sites (Cheonjeyeon Falls, Jusangjeolli cliffs). Choose the East if you want UNESCO peaks; the West if you want a denser concentration of flower gardens.

staticQuestions.question: Can I do this as a private tour?
staticQuestions.answer: We also run a private version of the hydrangea tour with hotel pickup anywhere on Jeju and customizable timing. Different product page; ask for the private option if you're a group of 4+ or want pickup outside Jeju City without the +₩70,000 surcharge.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to eastern Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: An active day with several walking-heavy stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a full day of moving around.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

staticQuestions.question: Can the tour run in rainy weather?
staticQuestions.answer: Light rain is fine; heavy rain may change a few stops.

itineraryStops.name: Hotel pickup (Jeju City)
itineraryStops.description: **Pickup is grouped across the four standard Jeju City pickup points** to keep the morning efficient before the eastbound drive: **Jeju International Airport (제주공항)** for airport-area guests, **Jeju City Hall (제주시청)** for the Yeon-dong / Nohyeong / Old Town hotel cluster, **Hagwi-dong (하귀동)** for the western Jeju City side, and **Dongmun Traditional Market (동문시장)** for the Tapdong/Sarabong waterfront area. The usual order is **Hagwi → Airport → City Hall → Dongmun → Route 1132 eastbound** toward Hamdeok. The small-group vehicle is guided by a licensed English-speaking guide or guide-driver. At the start of the day, the guide gives a weather note and hydrangea bloom update so photo time can be adjusted toward the strongest gardens that day.

itineraryStops.name: Hamdeok Seoubong Beach
itineraryStops.category: Coastal photo stop — emerald turquoise + Seoubong Oreum
itineraryStops.description: **Hamdeok Seoubong Beach (함덕 서우봉 해변)** is consistently ranked among **Jeju's top 3 beaches**, defined by the contrast between **white-sand beach (~500 m main stretch, 3 connected coves totaling ~900 m)** and the **adjacent volcanic cone Seoubong (서우봉, 'Cow Looking Like a Cow's Head' parasitic cone)** rising directly from the eastern end. The water's signature **emerald-turquoise color** comes from shallow coral-sand seabed reflecting through clear ocean — the Mediterranean-like contrast (rare in Korea) is what made Hamdeok one of the country's most photographed domestic beach destinations. The beach can absorb up to **50,000 daily visitors** at peak summer, but spring + autumn visits are typically uncrowded. **Seoubong Peak** (climbable ~1 hour to summit) wears **canola-yellow slopes in spring (March–April)** and offers a **panoramic eastward view to Manjanggul lava-tube area**. **Historical layer**: the sea cliffs immediately west of Seoubong hold **Imperial Japanese Army tunnels carved during WWII colonial occupation** — kamikaze suicide-boat shelters and ammunition stores, similar to the 18 caves at Seongsan Ilchulbong. The tunnels are publicly accessible (with signage explaining the historical context). The beach is the **central segment of Jeju Olle Trail Course 19 (조천 → 김녕)**, marked with the trail's signature blue-orange ribbons. **Cafe Delmoondo (델문도 카페)** sits directly on the beachfront — large terraces, sea-view, popular for photo and casual stops. **FREE admission**; the beach is **20 km / ~40-min drive from Jeju International Airport** on Route 1132.
highlights: Top-3 Jeju beach
Seoubong volcanic cone
Easy photo stop
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Morning emerald + first hydrangea-route warmup.** Hamdeok is the first stop because of three deliberate east-route reasons. (1) **Tide + light timing**: arriving 09:30 catches the morning low-side tide timing (most photogenic emerald-turquoise band before mid-morning swell + sun glare flatten contrast) — this is *the* signature Jeju east-coast photo, and getting it before the 10:30 coach-tour wave is a tangible guest win. (2) **Geographic highlight for the eastward arc**: Hamdeok sits ~14 km east of Jeju City on Route 1132 — the natural first pin on the coastal corridor that defines the day. Skipping it would force the day to either start with a 50-min highway run inland to Seongeup (visually dull) or open with a hydrangea garden (robbing the day of pacing variety). (3) **Hydrangea-route counterpoint**: Hamdeok's white sand + emerald water provides the day's only **non-floral** large-scenic highlight — every other substantive stop is hydrangea-coded or heritage-coded, so Hamdeok's role is to give the day visual breadth before the floral immersion.

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era folk heritage — UNESCO/National Folklore designation
itineraryStops.description: **Seongeup Folk Village (성읍 민속마을)** is a **living Joseon-era walled town founded in 1423** during the reign of **Sejong the Great** as Jeong-eui-hyeon (정의현), one of the three administrative seats of Joseon-era Jeju. The village served continuously as a **county administrative center until 1914** — nearly **500 years** — and retains **110 traditional structures** today within the original eupseong (읍성) walled-town footprint, designed on **Korean feng shui principles** typical of Joseon county capitals. The entire village is designated **Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea**, with **multiple individual buildings** carrying separate heritage status. At the village center stand **two ancient trees designated Natural Monuments of South Korea** — a **Saw-leaf Zelkova (느티나무)** and a **Japanese Hackberry (팽나무)**, both estimated **600+ years old** and revered as the village's spiritual highlight. Historical scars are visible: a **major fire in 1826 destroyed ~80 buildings**, and the village suffered fire damage **during the Jeju Uprising in 1948** — both events preserved as historical record on village signage. The **peak household count (historical, pre-modern) was ~1,500** — today the village remains inhabited by ~250 families, many actively maintaining traditional crafts. Drama filming credit: **'Dae Jang Geum (Jewel in the Palace)' (MBC, 2003–2004)**, the international K-drama hit. Local specialty: **Omegisul (오메기술)** — a **rice-based traditional Jeju liquor** brewed by **one family for multiple generations**, with the brewing demonstrated and tasted free of charge at the home-brewery within the village. **FREE admission**; outdoor walking dominates (~70% outdoor / 30% indoor for individual hanok visits).
highlights: Lived-in heritage village
Volcanic stone walls
Traditional kitchens and thatched roofs
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Heritage highlight and the day's only inland Joseon stop.** Three east-route reasons Seongeup belongs at 10:30. (1) **Geographic pivot**: Seongeup sits at the inland Pyoseon-myeon junction where the Hamdeok→Seongsan eastbound coastal arc detours south through the central highlands. Locking it as the morning's second substantive stop converts what would otherwise be 50 min of plain driving time into a heritage highlight with no detour penalty. (2) **Pace-and-tone counterpoint to the floral afternoon**: the rest of the day (Ilchul Land, Hueree) is hydrangea-immersive and garden-coded; Seongeup's stone-walled hanok lanes + pony-led Joseon administrative history give the route its only **deeply-cultural, unhurried walking** stop, preventing the day from collapsing into a floral-only photography tour. (3) **UNESCO-adjacent layer**: National Folklore Cultural Heritage No. 188 designation pairs naturally with the afternoon's UNESCO-triple Seongsan, building the east-route's heritage-plus-volcanism through-line that distinguishes it from the Tour 2 (southwest) garden-plus-coast route option.

itineraryStops.description: **Lunch is at a local Jeju-style restaurant in the Pyoseon-myeon / east-Seongsan corridor**, selected from a rotating shortlist of 3 family-run establishments that the operator has vetted for **English-friendly menus, dietary accommodation (vegetarian + halal-friendly chicken/seafood options on request), and local Jeju specialties** at fair group pricing. Typical lunch options include **galchi-jorim (braised silver hairtail)**, **heuk-dwaeji bulgogi (Jeju black pork bulgogi)**, **bibimbap with seasonal namul**, and **maeun-tang (spicy fish stew)**. The exact restaurant is selected the day before based on group size, dietary requests, and kitchen availability — guests are notified of the venue at the morning briefing. **Lunch cost is at guests' own expense** (typically ₩12,000–₩18,000 per person) and is **not included** in the tour fee. Beverages and rice/banchan refills are always included with the meal regardless of which dish is ordered.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO triple-designated tuff cone — climb to the rim
itineraryStops.description: **Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. **18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. **Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run **daily at 13:30 and 15:00** at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. **Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.

Placing it after lunch gives guests energy for the stone-step climb, then lets the afternoon ease into Ilchul Land and Hueree for the hydrangea-focused finale.

itineraryStops.name: Ilchul Land — Hydrangea Festival + Micheon Cave
itineraryStops.category: Hydrangea festival garden + lava-tube cave (June peak)
itineraryStops.description: **Ilchul Land (일출랜드)** is a **400,000 m² nature theme park** in Seongsan, opened **2002**, built around the **Micheon Cave (미천굴)** lava tube — a **1,700 m total length cave** with a **365 m artificially leveled walk-through section** open to visitors (~25-minute traverse). The cave is part of Jeju's **lava-tube system that shares geological lineage with Manjanggul Cave** — the UNESCO World Natural Heritage lava tube — and **functions as the practical alternative when Manjanggul closes for conservation maintenance or weather (typhoons, freezing)**. Where Manjanggul preserves its uneven natural floor as an authentic geological experience, Micheon's leveled walkway makes the cave **accessible to elderly visitors, families with strollers, and guests with mobility limitations** — a deliberate engineering choice that broadens the lava-tube experience to audiences who couldn't navigate Manjanggul. The interior is lit with **decorative seven-color lighting (gold, silver, bronze, red, black, blue, white)** highlighting cave formations — controversial among purists but practical for non-specialist tourist photography. Outside the cave, the park extends across **Subyeon Park, a reconstructed traditional Jeju village, Wild Flower Bonsai Garden, Cactus Greenhouse, Sculpture Street, and a Footbath Cafe (aroma foot spa)** — making this the **only Jeju attraction with an on-site Muslim prayer room** (a deliberate accessibility investment). Drama filming credit: **'Running Man' (런닝맨, SBS) Episode 89 (Alice-in-Wonderland themed, 2012)**. Admission: **Adult ₩12,000 / Youth ₩9,000 / Child ₩7,000**. Indoor/outdoor split is **30% indoor (cave + greenhouse)** / **70% outdoor (park grounds)** — useful if the day brings light rain or strong sun.

itineraryStops.name: Hueree Natural Park — Hydrangea highlight
itineraryStops.category: Hydrangea highlight + signature Jeju Black Pig Show
itineraryStops.description: **Hueree Natural Life Park (휴애리 자연생활공원)** is a year-round flower park on the southern foot of Hallasan, known for seasonal gardens with a Hallasan backdrop. The park rotates several flower events through the year, including plum blossoms, spring and summer hydrangeas, pink muhly, and camellias. **For the east-route hydrangea tour, the June season is the key draw**, with outdoor hydrangea paths and greenhouse areas that can still be useful if afternoon rain appears. The park is also known for the **Jeju Black Pig Show**, commonly scheduled several times a day; exact show times should be checked on the day. Other family-friendly areas may include a Jeju cultural village, traditional games, insect exhibits, wildflower areas, and small indoor photo spaces. Because much of Hueree is outdoors, the greenhouse, exhibit halls, and cafe-style spaces help the guide keep the stop comfortable in light rain or strong sun.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A seasonal east-Jeju loop built around the island's defining summer event. The hydrangea festivals at Hueree and Ilchul Land peak for roughly four weeks (late May to mid-July, peak mid-June) — narrow enough that most international travelers miss it without a planned tour. The route weaves the festival into the standard east-coast UNESCO circuit, so visitors who happen to be in Jeju during the bloom season get both the heritage day and the flowers in the same 9 hours.

bestFor: Active travelers comfortable with full days
Travelers who want UNESCO World Heritage stops
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: Two festivals in one day
items.detail: Jeju has four major hydrangea festivals (Hueree, Hallim Park, Camellia Hill, Ilchul Land). The east route includes two of them — Ilchul Land and Hueree — which together cover the broadest range of hydrangea varieties and photo settings. Pairing both is awkward by public bus.

items.label: UNESCO heritage stays in
items.detail: Many seasonal flower tours skip heritage sites in favor of more flower stops. This route keeps Seongsan Ilchulbong and Seongeup Folk Village on the itinerary — meaning even if you don't care about hydrangeas, the day still earns its keep as a standard east-Jeju UNESCO loop.

items.label: Built-in alternative when flowers underperform
items.detail: Hydrangea bloom timing varies year to year by 1–2 weeks. If you arrive a bit early or a bit late in the season, the heritage stops still highlight the day. The guide will swap in the strongest available flower fields based on the latest bloom conditions.

items.label: Hotel pickup beats self-driving for one-day visitors
items.detail: Many Jeju visitors are 1–2-day stopovers from Seoul. Renting a car requires an International Driving Permit and 30+ minutes of paperwork at the airport. A guided 9-hour day with hotel pickup means you arrive, sleep, get picked up, and see the best of east Jeju — no driving stress on the volcanic ridge roads.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Air-conditioned vehicle, licensed English/Chinese-speaking guide-driver, fuel, tolls, parking. Not included: attraction admissions (Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000, Hueree ₩13,000, Ilchul Land ₩12,000, Seongeup free), meals, tips. Total typical admission cost per person: ₩30,000–₩40,000.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free hotel pickup within Jeju City (around Jeju Airport area, downtown). Pickup outside Jeju City (Seogwipo, Aewol, Seongsan) incurs an additional ₩70,000 fee paid to the driver. Confirm pickup location at booking.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Hydrangea bloom timing
practicalAccordionItems.content: Peak bloom is typically late May to mid-July (peak mid-June), with some variation year to year. Check with the operator 1–2 weeks before your tour date for the latest bloom status. Outside this period, the route still runs but the hydrangea stops are off-season — consider the East Snow & Camellia winter tour or East Pink Muhly autumn tour instead.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. Seongsan Ilchulbong's rim climb is 30 minutes of stone steps with railings — tiring but well-marked. Visitors who skip it can walk the free coastal trail. Hueree, Ilchul Land, and the folk village are mostly flat. The Micheon Cave has uneven flooring and stairs.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable. Typhoon-related cancellations on Jeju (frequent in late summer) handled with full refund or reschedule.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Jeju in hydrangea season (June–July) is warm, humid, and prone to short rain showers. Temperatures of 22–28°C are typical, with high humidity. Bring sun protection and a light rain layer.

staticQuestions.question: When exactly do hydrangeas bloom on Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Typically late May to mid-July (peak mid-June), with peak around the last week of June. Bloom timing shifts ±1 week year to year based on spring rainfall and temperature. Check 1–2 weeks before your tour date for the latest update.

staticQuestions.question: Are admissions included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — budget about ₩30,000–₩40,000 per person for the day. Admissions: Seongsan Ilchulbong ₩5,000, Hueree ₩13,000, Ilchul Land ₩12,000, Seongeup Folk Village free.

staticQuestions.question: Why is Manjanggul closed?
staticQuestions.answer: Manjanggul Lava Tube is temporarily closed for safety repairs (status varies; check on booking date). The standard alternate is Micheon Cave at Ilchul Land, which is a similar lava-tube experience and is included in this route.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the West/South hydrangea tour?
staticQuestions.answer: This route covers Hueree + Ilchul Land hydrangeas plus east-coast UNESCO sites (Seongsan, Seongeup). The West/South tour (our West & South Hydrangea route) covers Hallim Park + Camellia Hill hydrangeas plus south-coast sites (Cheonjeyeon Falls, Jusangjeolli cliffs). Choose the East if you want UNESCO peaks; the West if you want a denser concentration of flower gardens.

staticQuestions.question: Can I do this as a private tour?
staticQuestions.answer: We also run a private version of the hydrangea tour with hotel pickup anywhere on Jeju and customizable timing. Different product page; ask for the private option if you're a group of 4+ or want pickup outside Jeju City without the +₩70,000 surcharge.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to eastern Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: An active day with several walking-heavy stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for families whose kids can handle a full day of moving around.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended only for active seniors comfortable with stairs and uneven ground.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 10 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

staticQuestions.question: Can the tour run in rainy weather?
staticQuestions.answer: Light rain is fine; heavy rain may change a few stops.
